page: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the EIASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 112 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 56 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 6.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the E112ias_v5 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression tests. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory performance is highly capable, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and small-block cached read bandwidth in the top 25%, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with PassMark CPU encryption tests registering in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets.

From a resource density perspective, the E112ias_v5 offers high compute and memory capacity within a single isolated environment, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring significant memory footprints without the overhead of dedicated physical hardware. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, which should be factored into architectural planning. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive and heavily multi-threaded workloads, such as large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance from the EIASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.525 GHz across 56 physical cores, this x86_64 architecture server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on PassMark CPU and stress-ng multi-core benchmarks. While it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. The instance exhibits strong memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and high-throughput database operations in the top 25%. Hardware-assisted cryptography via AES and VAES flags further enhances its security processing capabilities. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense, isolated workloads, making it highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated x86_64 instance featuring 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated AMD EPYC instance with 112 vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance database and memory workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity AMD EPYC server offering massive memory density and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory
Isolated virtual machine environment running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance in benchmark testing
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and multi-threaded workloads
